segments.text: Cold mattered because rubber does not respond the same way in cold.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: A seal that moves quickly when warm can respond more slowly when cold.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: In this joint, timing was not a detail.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: Timing was the safety margin.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: If the seals failed, there was no graceful backup.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: Hot gas could burn through the booster casing, reach surrounding structure, and turn a local leak into a vehicle failure.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: The o-ring problem had been showing itself before Challenger.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: Post-flight inspections found erosion on the primary seal.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: In some cases, gas had blown past it and left evidence deeper in the joint.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: Engineers documented the damage.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: They measured it.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: They reported it.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: The dangerous part is what happened next.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: Because the mission succeeded, because the vehicles came home, damage could be processed as evidence that the system had tolerated the condition.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: A damaged component in a surviving system can be read two ways.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: One reading says,
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: We were closer to failure than we should have been, so we stop and fix the condition before we repeat it.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: The other says, the vehicle survived, the margin held, and the risk can be carried while improvement work continues.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: The second reading became easier each time the shuttle flew and returned.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: What had started as a serious anomaly gradually became part of the operating history.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: The O-ring warning did not disappear.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: It moved.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: That is how a warning can die inside a large organization, not through silence, through translation.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: It starts when an engineer sees something wrong.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: The data is specific.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: The language is uncomfortable.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: Erosion.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: Blow by.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: Heat effects.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: a seal that did not behave the way the design assumed.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: Precise evidence has force.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: Then the evidence becomes a discussion item.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: It enters a meeting with dozens of other concerns, other anomalies, other systems, other deadlines.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: The context broadens, and the urgency flattens.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: From there, the discussion becomes a judgment call.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: Does this affect the next flight?
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: The answer depends on the question the room decides to ask.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: If the question is, can we prove this will cause a failure?
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: The answer is usually no.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: Predicting a specific loss on a specific flight is hard.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: If the question is, can we prove this will not fail?
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: The answer is also no.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00

segments.text: But the first question keeps the schedule alive.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00
